at 201-220 bars sma200Slope returns null (needs >=221) so slopePositive is false and a sub--10% dip emits a confident FALLING_KNIFE SELL (82-95%) from missing-slope data, contradicting the dev==null fail-closed intent; reachable via resolveBacktestSignal (core.ts:238 i=200, slice 0..i+1) on each instrument's first ~20 evaluated bars; long-only so SELL-when-flat is a no-op, effect is spurious early exits if already holding that early

entry double-counts the 2bps open friction: entryPrice = nextOpen*(1+ENTRY_SLIPPAGE_BPS=2bps) AND txCost = 11bps/side which already includes a 2bps slippage component (executionModel slippageBpsPerSide) -> entry ~13bps vs the 11bps/side SSOT, round-trip ~24 vs 22bps. Exit (closePosition) is clean at 11bps. Confirmed live in core.ts backtestInstrument; reconcile to a single 2bps source (drop the price bump or zero the txCost slippage leg)

maxHoldDays is measured in UNION-calendar steps not the instrument's trading days: holdDays = currentIdx - entryIdx (exitRules.ts:252) is fed di = the union date index (portfolioBacktest builds a union of all tickers' trading dates at :151, passes di at :316, sets entryIdx: di at :464). An instrument that does not trade on some union dates (mixed equity/crypto calendars, halts, missing bars) has its hold window inflated by other tickers' sessions -> time_exit fires on the wrong horizon. DORMANT: portfolioBacktest is a dev script (scripts/portfolio-backtest.ts), in no API route and no CI gate, so nothing is published. Fix = count per-instrument bars held; needs a portfolio test (none exists)

trailing-stop peak ratchets on CLOSE only: updatePosition(pos, row.close) sets highestPrice from the close, never the intra-bar high, so the trail anchor (highestPrice*(1-trailingStopPct), exitRules.ts:244) understates the true peak -> looser/late trailing exits. NOTE: arguably a DELIBERATE conservative choice (close-based trailing avoids intra-bar whipsaw), and there is NO same-bar look-ahead (checkExitConditions receives the pre-update pos, so today's close cannot move today's trail). DORMANT (portfolioBacktest, not live/published). Decision: keep close-based vs switch to high-based ratchet

live-vs-backtest inconsistency: buildLiveInstrumentSignal calls resolveBacktestSignal with NO sectorGates argument, while benchmarkLabel.ts passes sectorGatesForTicker(ticker)=getProfileForTicker when enhanced is on. sectorGates only affect the enhanced path, which is OFF in production (featureFlags.ts:15 returns false when NODE_ENV=production), so live and backtest are identical TODAY. If enhanced is ever enabled, the live desk signal would skip sector gating that the backtest applies -> divergence on gated tickers. DORMANT (enhanced is research-only, slated retire-or-invest). Fix = pass the same gates (no-op in prod); verify intent when enhanced is revisited

portfolio alpha compares MISMATCHED windows: alpha = truePortfolioReturn - bnhAvg, where truePortfolioReturn is the END-ALIGNED COMMON (min-length) window combine but bnhAvg = mean of each instrument's FULL-history bnhReturn (core.ts:422 computeBuyAndHoldReturn over all rows). For instruments of unequal length the portfolio leg spans fewer bars than the B&H leg -> alpha is apples-to-oranges. LIVE path (app/api/backtest/route.ts -> aggregatePortfolio) and alpha is shown in the backtest UI. LATENT on the current 56-file dataset (all 1255 bars -> common==full -> alpha currently correct), but /api/backtest accepts arbitrary tickers so mixed-length sets are reachable. Fix = compute bnhAvg over the same end-aligned common window (or compute portfolio return over each instrument's own window); NOT covered by the CI benchmark (which uses benchmarkLabel, not aggregatePortfolio) so a fix cannot be WR-gated
status: open-owner-gated (changes published UI alpha; no benchmark gate)

SSOT consistency: bollingerArray rejects period<2 (returns all-NaN, F2.9 — at period=1 sample variance is identically 0 and the bands collapse to the price line) but bollingerLatest only checks closes.length<period, so period=1 returns degenerate collapsed bands instead of null. Not a live bug (every caller uses the default period=20; unreachable), but the two SSOT entry points should treat period<2 identically. Fix = add `|| period < 2` to the bollingerLatest guard. Rest of indicators.ts VERIFIED CLEAN (all 25 primitives correct + finite/div0-guarded; prior bugs fixed)

relativeStrengthVsBenchmark uses INDEX-based lookback (closes[len-22]/[len-64]/[len-127] vs spy[len-22]/[len-64]/[len-127]) and only documents 'same date alignment expected' — it does NOT enforce it (unlike the sibling alignCloses which aligns by YYYY-MM-DD key). A ticker with a halt-gap or shorter history has its index-N-ago bar on a DIFFERENT calendar date than SPY's, so the ratio %-change (pct1m/3m/6m) and the RS ranking are computed across mismatched dates. LIVE (app/api/sector-rotation + analytics) but mitigated: current callers feed same-calendar US equities (sector ETFs; single ticker vs SPY) so dates line up. Fix = align by date (alignCloses) before the index lookbacks, or intersect the date sets

ewmaVolForecast annualizes with Math.sqrt(252), but this is the BTC conditional-vol forecast: served LIVE by app/api/conditional-vol/[ticker] and shown in BtcQuantLab, and per the 2026-06-10 review the EWMA fallback is what is actually displayed (no /garch sidecar route wired; fetchGarchForecast always falls back). BTC trades 365 days/yr, so sqrt(252) understates the BTC conditional vol by sqrt(365/252)-1 ~= 17% (same class as F-12 walkForward, but garch IS published/crypto-reachable unlike Q16/Q17 whose caller was equity-only). Fix = parameterize annualization (like indicators.sharpeRatio) and pass tradingDaysPerYear=365 for crypto. Changes a displayed API number and is NOT covered by the CI benchmark (which uses benchmarkLabel) so a fix cannot be WR-gated. Also: btc-indicators.ts reimplements calcEMA/calcRSI/calcMACD/calcATR/calcOBV/calcADX separately from the indicators.ts SSOT (drift risk; architectural dup)
status: open-owner-gated (changes published crypto vol; no benchmark gate)

KV rate-limiter EXPIRE...NX self-heal swallows its own failure (.catch(()=>{})) and is gated on count===1 so it never re-fires. The narrow conjunction 'the key expired in the SET-NX->INCR gap AND the single heal fetch fails' can strand a TTL-less counter = a PERMANENT per-IP block — the exact failure SET-NX was meant to eliminate. LIVE (all API routes). Fix = check the heal response .ok and fall back to checkRateLimitMemory, or issue EXPIRE unconditionally. Source-verified on origin/main

line: boundary finite-guard gaps (defense-in-depth) — several live routes return upstream/sidecar/provider numbers without mirroring the analytics route's finiteOrNull boundary guard, so a non-finite slips to JSON and serializes to null silently: (1) regime/[ticker]:42 + conditional-vol/[ticker]:41 pass sidecar JSON verbatim (regimeHmmClient.ts:59 / garchClient.ts:71 unvalidated); (2) garchClient.ts EWMA log(0)=-Inf when a close is <=0 (dataLoader filters finite but NOT >0); (3) stream/[ticker]:51-57 null-check not finite-check on the quote; (4) chart/[ticker]:205-211 OHLC finite-filter omits volume. All LOW (no user-controlled trigger; first-party sidecars; sanitized data has 0 non-finite). Fix = mirror finiteOrNull at each boundary + a >0 close filter. Source-verified by parallel agents + coordinator on origin/main

the chart cached-hit branch (:64) omits the CDN-Cache-Control header that the fresh-response branch sets (:121-122) — cached responses lose the intended CDN cache directive. LIVE. Cosmetic/perf, no correctness or security impact. Source-verified on origin/main

news-brief id was Buffer.from(link).toString('base64').slice(0,16) — base64 encodes input in independent 3-byte groups so 16 chars = exactly the first 12 bytes of link. Every Yahoo news link shares a 12-byte prefix (https://fina for finance.yahoo.com, https://www. for the rest) so ids collapsed to one value per domain. Client renders briefs with key={brief.id} (app/briefs/page.tsx:75) -> duplicate React keys -> dropped/mis-keyed cards + console error. LIVE on /briefs. FIXED: extracted newsBriefId(link) (lib/api/briefId.ts) encoding the FULL link with base64url (bijection -> zero collisions); +__tests__/api/briefId.test.ts (5 cases incl. proving the old truncation collides). Benchmark-neutral; no schema change (id stays a string)
status: FIXED 2026-06-25 (PR #72 -> main 6ea4293; tsc clean; test 5/5; prod smoke PASS)

Hidden from tsc by the (q as Record<string,unknown>) casts. DISPLAY-ONLY on the sector brief (no signal/published-metric path); verified against node_modules/yahoo-finance2 quote.d.ts. Fix = regularMarketVolume + averageDailyVolume3Month

one_line: provider OHLC input validation is INCONSISTENT and individually-INCOMPLETE across all three implementors: (1) PolygonProvider.fetchDaily maps r.o/r.h/r.l/r.c with NO finite/positive guard at all; (2) AlphaVantage parseFiniteOrNull guards non-finite but lets a literal 0/negative through (Number.isFinite(0)===true) — its doc-comment misleadingly claims it stops 0-price injection, which it does NOT (it only stops missing->0 coercion); (3) Yahoo skips close==null||close<=0 but a NaN close slips (NaN<=0 is false). A robust provider should skip any bar with a non-finite OR <=0 price. Unify all three to finite-AND-positive (skip the bar). DORMANT (the provider layer has zero prod callers — see A5-DORMANCY) so no live impact; benchmark-neutral. NOT shipping a Polygon-only guard because that would make Polygon stricter than its two siblings = trading one asymmetry for another in dead code
status: MOOT 2026-06-27 — provider layer DELETED (PR #73); the dead code carrying this is gone

one_line: fetchDaily builds each bar from raw open/high/low (fields 1./2./3.) but the ADJUSTED close (field 5. adjusted close), per the AlphaVantage TIME_SERIES_DAILY_ADJUSTED docs where fields 1-4 are as-traded/raw and field 5 is split+dividend adjusted. Across a split or large dividend, the raw OHL are on a different price scale than the adjusted close, so the bar's low can exceed its close (OHLC invariant low<=close<=high violated) and indicators (ATR/returns) see a discontinuity. NOTE: claim rests on AlphaVantage API docs, not on repo types (the response is an untyped Record<string,string> so it is NOT verifiable against a .d.ts like A4-1 was). DORMANT (provider not wired). Fix = scale OHL by factor = adjustedClose/rawClose (field 4. close) so all four are on the adjusted scale, or use raw close (4.) for raw bars
citation: alphavantage.ts:73-76 (1./2./3. raw + 5. adjusted close); per AlphaVantage docs

one_line: _fetchViaCsv (:79) and _fetchViaApi (:99) parse non-missing observation values with parseFloat() and NO finite guard, so a malformed (non-'.' non-numeric) value yields NaN in observations.value (typed number|null). FRED uses '.' for missing (correctly mapped to null), so a NaN requires malformed upstream data = near-zero likelihood, and the live rf consumer (riskFreeRate.ts, Q24) walks backward for the latest non-missing with a val>0 guard so it would skip a NaN anyway. DORMANT-adjacent + benign. Fix = Number.isFinite(parseFloat(val)) ? parsed : null (symmetric to the '.' handling)

CSP-nonce mechanism (Q-040-NEW, self-labelled 'partial') is INCOMPLETE scaffolding and is a LANDMINE on the planned owner ENV-CSP flip. middleware generates a per-request nonce and sets x-nonce on the request, but (a) x-nonce has ZERO consumers repo-wide (grep app/components/lib/hooks — nothing reads it), and (b) when QUANTAN_CSP_ENFORCE=1 the enforced Content-Security-Policy uses script-src 'self' 'nonce-{X}' with NO 'unsafe-inline', set only on the RESPONSE. Next.js App Router always emits inline bootstrap/hydration scripts (which is why next.config's Report-Only policy carries 'unsafe-inline'); with no nonce propagation to those scripts (no x-nonce reader; CSP not on the request headers, which is how Next propagates a nonce per its App-Router CSP pattern), flipping QUANTAN_CSP_ENFORCE=1 would BLOCK Next's own inline scripts -> blank/broken app. The break is overdetermined (enforced nonce-only + zero propagation) regardless of the exact Next mechanism. DORMANT today (flag off -> zero live impact; live CSP is Report-Only, app fine). DISPOSITION (owner, A5-DORMANCY-shaped but lean COMPLETE not delete — the Report-Only->enforce plan shows intent to ship strict CSP): finish the nonce path (consume x-nonce in the root layout to nonce Next's scripts, and/or set the nonce'd CSP on the REQUEST headers) OR remove the dead nonce scaffolding if abandoning strict CSP. VALIDATION: set QUANTAN_CSP_ENFORCE=1 on a PREVIEW deploy + load with console open -> CSP violations on Next inline scripts confirm the break (no prod touch)
status: open-owner-gated — LINKED to the 'ENV-CSP flip (7-day Report-Only -> enforce)' backlog item: do NOT flip QUANTAN_CSP_ENFORCE=1 until the nonce path is completed/validated on a preview deploy

Behaviour-preserving: visible EMAs always get data (effect re-runs on tick via `candles` + on toggle via indicatorsProp/visSerialised); hidden skipped; crosshair reads candle OHLCV only; toggle-on re-runs the effect. Frontend, OFF the WR path -> benchmark-neutral
